steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: IPMN-related complication (cyst rupture, infected cyst, malignant transformation with local invasion); pancreatic abscess or infected peripancreatic fluid collection; lymphoma or other malignancy given night sweats, weight-related symptoms, and known pancreatic tail lesion
ensemble.information_gap: The non-contrast CT in the ED showed interval increase in IPMN size with adjacent fat-stranding, but the extent of peripancreatic inflammation, presence of necrosis, infected fluid collection, ductal dilation, or vascular involvement cannot be fully characterized without IV contrast — and CKD limits but does not absolutely preclude contrast use at this creatinine level
ensemble.expected_finding: Enhancement pattern of the pancreatic parenchyma to assess for necrosis, characterization of the IPMN (size, wall enhancement, solid component, main duct involvement), presence of a peripancreatic fluid collection or abscess, and assessment of the main pancreatic duct for dilation consistent with obstructive pancreatitis from the IPMN
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The non-contrast ED CT identified an enlarging IPMN with fat-stranding but cannot characterize necrosis, ductal obstruction, vascular involvement, or infected collections — all of which directly drive management decisions; a contrast-enhanced CT is the standard next step to assess severity and guide intervention.
grounding: History: 'CT ABD/PELVIS: Interval increase in size of IPMN with new adjacent fat-stranding, consistent with inflammation'
History: 'fevers as high as ___ F at home' and 'night sweats several times over the last few weeks that involved soaking his pajamas and the sheets'
Labs: 'White Blood Cells: 12.6 K/uL' with 'Absolute Neutrophil Count: 8.9 K/uL' — leukocytosis with neutrophilia
Labs: 'Lactate: 2.0 mmol/L' — at upper limit of normal, suggesting early physiologic stress
Labs: 'Creatinine: 1.5 mg/dL' with 'Estimated GFR = 48' — CKD III, contrast use requires risk-benefit consideration but does not absolutely contraindicate
History: 'He is scheduled for endoscopy with FNA on ___' — known IPMN under active workup for malignant potential
ensemble.reasoning: The ED non-contrast CT confirmed an enlarging pancreatic tail IPMN with peripancreatic fat-stranding, but I cannot determine whether there is pancreatic necrosis, an infected fluid collection, main pancreatic duct obstruction, or vascular involvement without IV contrast enhancement. Given the fever, leukocytosis, mildly elevated lipase, borderline lactate, and a known IPMN with malignant potential, a contrast-enhanced CT is essential to characterize the severity of the inflammatory process, identify any drainable collection or ductal pathology, and screen for features of malignant transformation — all of which will directly determine whether this patient needs endoscopic, interventional radiology, or surgical management.

vindication.actual_finding: Contrast-enhanced CT showed the pancreatic tail IPMN has significantly enlarged to 5.3 x 4.7 x 4.3 cm (from 3.2 x 2 x 2.3 cm) with new adjacent fat stranding, no ductal dilation of the main pancreatic duct proximally, no necrosis of the remaining parenchyma, no peripancreatic fluid collection or abscess, and no vascular involvement.
vindication.relation_to_expectation: The contrast study addressed all the key expected findings — parenchymal enhancement pattern (no necrosis), IPMN characterization (size and growth confirmed), absence of fluid collection/abscess, and main pancreatic duct status (not dilated) — thereby resolving the information gap the physician identified.
